rulings.Case Title: MALCOLM MEYERS VS. CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 18, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANT JOHN FERGUSON's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted by the moving party without need for any appearances. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL HARRIS VS. ALBERT A LEVY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration Of Stipulated Settleemtn Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 18, 2012, Line 2, PLAINTIFF MICHAEL HARRIS' Motion For Reconsideration Of Stipulated Settleemtn Agreement. Denied. The motion is untimely per Code of Civil Procedure 1008. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 18, 2012, Line 3, PLAINTIFF PEGASTAFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY'S FURTHER RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY. rulings.Case Title: ANNA FLIDER VS. JOSEPH DUGAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Relief Fron Judgmnt
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 18, 2012, Line 1. DEFENDANT NOE STREET PROPERTY, L.P.'S Motion For Relief Fron Judgment. Pro Tem Judge Trent Thornley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN ENG VS. SEAN BUCKLEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Moti And Motion To Require Vexatious Litigant To Provide Security Pursuant To Ccp 391.1; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 18, 2012, Line 6, DEFENDANT BUCKLEY REAL ESTATE, INC.'s Motion To Require Vexatious Litigant To Provide Security Pursuant To Ccp 391.1. Granted. No opposition filed. Moving defendant has shown that plaintiff has been declared a vexatious litigant and plaintiff's case lacks merit. Plaintiff must post a bond in the amount of $20,000 within fifteen days of notice of entry of the order being mailed to him. The case is stayed pending the posting of the bond. If the bond is not timely posted, moving defendant may seek dismissal per an ex parte application with the requisite notice to the plaintiff. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: QING LIU MO VS. LILLIAN MOCK et al

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 18, 2012, Line 22, PLAINTIFF QING MO's DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with fifteen days leave to amend. Defendants have requested leave to amend. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. . The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK MYERS VS. JENNY PEI LIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re Tro/Prelminary Injunction; Opposition Due 7/13/12; No Reply
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 18, 2012, Line 23, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE TRO/PRELMINARY INJUNCTION. Granted. Plaintiff and any other nonparty acting in concert with Plaintiff are enjoined from entering into the private property areas of 201-205 Ninth Street, San Francisco, California, 94103, without complying with the California Code of Civil Procedure sections applicable to demands for inspection of property. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SEAN DEVRIES VS. APPLE, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 18, 2012, Line 24, DEFENDANT APPLE, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with fifteen days leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead an actionable misrepresentation or a nondisclosure based on an actual duty to disclose. In amending his complaint, plaintiff should keep in mind that damages, as opposed to restitution, cannot be recovered for violations of the UCL. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
